items.titleZh: 开源RISC-V平台从RTL到芯片培训芯片设计师（苏黎世联邦理工学院、lowRISC、博洛尼亚大学）
items.descriptionZh:
瑞士苏黎世联邦理工学院（ETH Zurich）、lowRISC 和博洛尼亚大学（University of Bologna）的研究人员联合发表了一篇题为《Croc：基于领域专用全流程开源硅芯片培养下一代芯片设计师》的技术论文，提出了一套名为 **Croc** 的开源、可定制 RISC-V SoC 平台及配套教学流程。该平台的核心目标是让芯片设计学生能够以“领域专用”（Domain-Specific）的方式，完整经历从架构设计、RTL 开发到物理设计（Physical Design）乃至最终流片的全链条实践，从而填补传统芯片设计教育中理论与实际流片经验之间的鸿沟。

**背景与上下文**  
随着摩尔定律放缓，领域专用架构（如 AI 加速器、IoT 处理器）成为提升性能与能效的关键。然而，芯片设计教育长期面临两大痛点：一是商业 EDA 工具成本高昂且封闭，学生难以接触完整的设计流程；二是从 RTL 到 GDS（最终版图）的物理设计环节往往被简化为理论讲授，缺乏真实的流片实践。Croc 应运而生，它基于开放指令集 RISC-V，利用完全开源的 EDA 工具链（如 OpenROAD、Yosys、Verilator 等），让学生能在低成本甚至零成本前提下体验从架构探索到芯片生成的完整路径。

**核心技术原理与创新点**  
Croc 的核心是一个专为教学优化的 RISC-V 内核——**Croc Core**，它是一个 32 位、支持 RV32IMC 指令集（含乘除法和压缩指令）的单发射微控制器级处理器。关键创新包括：  
1. **领域专用定制化**：Croc 平台设计为“可组合”——学生可以围绕 Croc Core 添加自定义协处理器、加速器或外设（如用于神经网络推理的 MAC 单元、信号处理单元等），并以 SoC 形式集成。这鼓励学生在架构层面思考专用化设计。  
2. **端到端开源流程**：教学流程覆盖架构级性能评估（使用 Spike/riscv-tests）、RTL 设计（SystemVerilog）、逻辑综合（Yosys）、物理设计（OpenROAD）以及最终生成 GDSII 版图。所有工具均为开源，且配置了教学友好的脚本和文档，降低学习曲线。  
3. **面向流片的设计约束**：Croc 专门针对 180nm 或 130nm 等成熟工艺节点（如 SkyWater 130nm 开源 PDK）进行优化，该节点成本低、设计规则简单，适合教学流片。学生能实际将自己设计的芯片送到代工厂（如通过 Efabless 或 MPW 项目），获得真实芯片。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
论文公开了 Croc Core 的部分指标（基于 SkyWater 130nm 工艺）：  
- 面积：约 0.1 mm²（不含缓存和协处理器）  
- 最大运行频率：约 50 MHz（在典型电压下）  
- 功耗：数 mW 量级  
- 性能：约 1.5 DMIPS/MHz，与同类开源内核（如 PicoRV32）相当，但拥有更灵活的 SoC 总线接口（支持 AXI4-Lite 或 TileLink）和更低面积成本。  

与商业教学 SoC（如 ARM Cortex-M0）相比，Croc 仅需 1/10 甚至更低的流片成本，且学生能完全透明地查看和修改所有 RTL 及物理设计文件。论文还展示了多个教学案例：一个添加了 AES 硬件加速器的同学完成的设计，在面积仅增加 15% 的情况下，AES-128 加密速度提升约 20 倍。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
1. **教育领域**：Croc 提供了一种可复用的开源课程模板，适合研究生级高级数字设计、VLSI 或计算机体系结构课程。学生可以在一个学期内完成从零到流片的完整项目，真正理解设计决策对性能、功耗、面积的物理影响。  
2. **科研与原型验证**：由于开源和可定制，研究人员可以基于 Croc 快速验证新的领域专用架构（如边缘 AI 加速器或传感器融合处理器），并低成本流片获取实测数据，避免依赖模拟器的不确定性。  
3. **开源硬件生态**：Croc 与 RISC-V 社区紧密结合，其 SoC 设计可被其他开源项目重用（如作为 IoT 节点控制器的基带芯片），进一步推动“开源硅”的民主化。  

总之，Croc 不仅是一个教学工具，更是一个弥合芯片设计教育与工业实践之间的桥梁。它通过完全开源、领域专用、端到端可流片的路径，让下一代芯片设计师不仅掌握理论，更拥有亲手制造硅芯片的真实经验，这对于应对未来多样化、专用化芯片的需求至关重要。

items.title: Legacy Nvidia RTX 3060 12GB returns to retail five years after original launch, priced at $339 — resurrected GPU strategy that Jensen called a 'good idea' apparently comes to fruition
items.description: After Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said that "it's a good idea" to consider re-introducing older GPUs made on trailing process nodes, the five-year-old RTX 3060 is back on e-tailer shelves, priced at $339.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 6 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Back at CES 2026, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said that "it's a good idea" to consider reintroducing older GeForce RTX graphics cards with GPUs from trailing-edge nodes to alleviate pricing and availability concerns around cutting-edge RTX 50-series products. It appears that the idea may now have become reality. After a hiatus that began late last year, RTX 3060 12GB cards (first launched in early 2021) are beginning to reappear as new stock at e-tailers.We've spotted a Gigabyte RTX 3060 12GB card available direct from new stock at Newegg for $339.99, or just $10 above its MSRP from over five years ago. This Windforce card is a straightforward dual-fan model with no frills, but tellingly, it carries a rare Rev2.0 suffix, directly indicating a revision or new model of some kind. That revision number aside, it's identical (as far as we can tell) to the Rev1.0 card that presumably appeared nearer to the RTX 3060 12GB's launch.It appears that different board partners are working to quietly bring back the RTX 3060 12GB worldwide, as ComputerBase notes the availability of an Asus Dual card in the European market. We wouldn't be surprised if that product makes its way Stateside, as well.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Gamers may have hoped that the reintroduction of an older product from a foundry and a process node that isn't capacity-constrained (namely Samsung 8nm), as well as an older memory technology, would have resulted in some pricing relief, but that doesn't appear to be the case.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)The RTX 3060 12GB is reappearing for just a few bucks less than the far superior RTX 5060, which offers both stronger baseline performance and unfettered performance with the latest DLSS 4.5 upscaling tech. Heck, even the entry-level RTX 5050 outpaces the RTX 3060 in our most recent tests.The 3060's extra 4GB of VRAM compared to those products only comes into play with settings and resolutions where its shader horsepower is already inadequate to produce playable frame rates, so it's more of a psychological advantage than a practical one.So why this product and why now? As AI wafer demand on cutting-edge nodes only grows, it's possible that simple economics dictate that the opportunity cost of producing entry-level Blackwell GPUs for the desktop is simply too great compared to the revenue and margin opportunities of making data center Rubin GPUs for the countless Vera Rubin NVL72 racks that the company is doubtless planning to ship later this year.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIn the event that Nvidia does prioritize datacenter products over consumer ones, the already elevated prices of entry-level RTX 50-series cards might give way to a situation where one simply can't buy these lower-priced, lower-margin products at any cost. And in that event, the aging RTX 3060 could be one of the few sub-$350 options available to gamers who need to build a new system or upgrade from an aging graphics card.And frankly, that's a poor outcome for gamers, as the RTX 3060 can't run the latest DLSS 4.5 upscaling model at its full potential due to the lack of proper FP8 acceleration on older Turing and Ampere GPUs. And it can't run DLSS Frame Generation at all, leaving gamers to deal with cross-platform solutions like FSR and XeSS frame generation from other vendors. Those technologies are incredibly useful tools in getting the best experience out of modern games, and if they're only available on a smaller subset of more expensive GPUs, that further stratifies the already stratified modern PC gaming experience.We've reached out to Gigabyte and Nvidia for comment on the reappearance of the RTX 3060 and will report what we learn if we hear back. items.titleZh: 经典Nvidia RTX 3060 12GB在首次推出五年后重返零售市场，售价339美元——黄仁勋曾称为“好主意”的复活GPU策略显然已取得成果
items.descriptionZh:
在CES 2026大会上，英伟达CEO黄仁勋曾表示，考虑重新推出基于较落后制程节点的旧款GeForce RTX显卡，以缓解RTX 50系列显卡因尖端制程产能紧张带来的价格和供应问题。如今这一想法似乎已成为现实：在去年底短暂停产后，RTX 3060 12GB显卡（最初于2021年初发布）正以新库存的形式重新出现在零售商货架上。

我们在Newegg上发现了一款技嘉RTX 3060 12GB Windforce双风扇显卡，售价339.99美元，仅比五年前的建议零售价高出10美元。值得注意的是，该卡带有罕见的Rev2.0后缀，直接表明这是一款修订版或新型号。除版本号外，它与接近首发时出现的Rev1.0版本基本一致。此外，ComputerBase报道称，欧洲市场已出现华硕Dual版本，预计该产品也将进入美国市场。

深入分析技术层面，这款复刻显卡基于三星8nm工艺（非容量受限的尖端节点），并沿用较旧的内存技术。理论上这应能带来价格优势，但现实并非如此——其售价仅比性能远胜于它的RTX 5060低几美元。RTX 5060不仅拥有更强的基线性能，还完整支持最新的DLSS 4.5超分辨率技术；即便是入门级的RTX 5050，在最新测试中也已超越RTX 3060。RTX 3060额外的4GB显存优势，仅在设置和分辨率已使着色器算力无法提供可玩帧率时才能体现，更多是心理上的慰藉而非实际收益。

为何此时复活这款老产品？根本原因在于尖端制程上AI晶圆需求持续攀升。对英伟达而言，生产入门级Blackwell架构桌面GPU的机会成本过高，远不及将产能用于数据中心Rubin GPU（服务于无数Vera Rubin NVL72机架）所带来的收入和利润率。若英伟达真将数据中心产品优先级置于消费级之上，本就昂贵的RTX 50系列入门卡可能面临“有钱也买不到”的局面。此时，老旧的RTX 3060将成为少数售价低于350美元的选项，满足玩家组装新机或升级老显卡的需求。

然而，这对玩家而言是个糟糕的结果。RTX 3060因缺乏对老图灵和安培架构上FP8加速的原生支持，无法以完整潜力运行最新的DLSS 4.5模型，更无法运行DLSS帧生成功能。玩家只能依赖跨平台方案如FSR和XeSS帧生成。这些技术是充分发挥现代游戏体验的关键工具，若仅限少数更昂贵的GPU使用，将进一步加剧本已分层的PC游戏体验。我们已联系技嘉和英伟达就RTX 3060重新上市一事置评，待收到回复后将进一步报道。

items.title: Claude Meets Blackwell Ultra: Anthropic’s Models Now Run on NVIDIA GB300 in Azure
items.description: Anthropic’s Claude models in Microsoft Foundry — hosted on Microsoft Azure and running on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs — are now generally available, giving Azure-native enterprises a powerful new way to build autonomous and domain-specific AI agents. As agentic AI continues to drive enterprise innovation and becomes more autonomous, organizations need access to computing […]
items.content: Anthropic’s Claude models in Microsoft Foundry — hosted on Microsoft Azure and running on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs — are now generally available, giving Azure-native enterprises a powerful new way to build autonomous and domain-specific AI agents. As agentic AI continues to drive enterprise innovation and becomes more autonomous, organizations need access to computing power to build and deploy specialized agents to accelerate essential business tasks. And having great inference performance and efficiency reduces total cost of ownership and drives positive company results. With Claude in Foundry running on NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 systems with NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand networking, enterprises can now build and run more powerful agentic systems, including autonomous and specialized sub-agents that can work across business domains to perform advanced tasks. A Growing Partnership NVIDIA is working with Anthropic to extend developer capabilities by integrating NVIDIA tools into the Anthropic stack. That integration enables enterprises to give Claude agents domain-specific abilities. Through NVIDIA verified agent skills, enabled by access to NVIDIA accelerated computing, enterprises can embed AI agents deeply into their business and use them as the operating system for the organization. Enterprises can run Claude agents on Azure by using the NVIDIA Secure Agent Workspace Reference Design. It provides a blueprint for running autonomous agents in a governed environment where identity, network access, credentials and runtime policy are controlled at the infrastructure level. Claude in Microsoft Foundry accelerated by NVIDIA GB300 GPUs on Azure builds on the strategic partnership Microsoft, NVIDIA and Anthropic announced in November to expand enterprise access to Claude and offer Anthropic models on NVIDIA accelerated computing. Get started by visiting Claude in Microsoft Foundry and learn more in Foundry documentation.
items.author: Dave Salvator
items.titleZh: Claude 遇见 Blackwell Ultra：Anthropic 模型现已可在 Azure 上的 NVIDIA GB300 运行
items.descriptionZh:
Anthropic 的 Claude 模型现已在 Microsoft Foundry 中正式全面可用，该服务托管于 Microsoft Azure 之上，并运行于 NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPU。这为原生使用 Azure 的企业提供了一种强大的新方式，以构建自主且面向特定领域的 AI 智能体。随着智能体 AI 持续推动企业创新并变得更加自主，企业需要获取强大的计算能力来构建和部署专用智能体，从而加速关键业务任务。卓越的推理性能和效率能够降低总拥有成本，并推动积极的企业成果。通过让 Claude 运行在基于 NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 系统、配备 NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand 网络的 Foundry 平台中，企业现在可以构建和运行更强大的智能体系统，包括能够在不同业务领域协作执行高级任务的自主子智能体和专用子智能体。

### 不断深化的合作
NVIDIA 正与 Anthropic 合作，通过将 NVIDIA 工具集成到 Anthropic 技术栈中来扩展开发者能力。这种集成使企业能够赋予 Claude 智能体特定领域的能力。借助通过 NVIDIA 加速计算实现的 NVIDIA 已验证智能体技能，企业可以将 AI 智能体深度嵌入其业务中，并将其作为组织的操作系统来使用。企业可以通过使用 NVIDIA 安全智能体工作空间参考设计，在 Azure 上运行 Claude 智能体。该参考设计提供了一个蓝图，用于在受治理的环境中运行自主智能体，在基础设施层面控制身份、网络访问、凭据和运行时策略。

### 战略合作伙伴关系的延伸
在 Microsoft Foundry 中由 NVIDIA GB300 GPU 加速的 Claude，建立在 Microsoft、NVIDIA 和 Anthropic 于去年 11 月宣布的战略合作伙伴关系之上，该合作旨在扩大企业对 Claude 的访问，并在 NVIDIA 加速计算上提供 Anthropic 模型。入门方式：访问 Microsoft Foundry 中的 Claude，并查阅 Foundry 文档了解更多信息。

items.title: Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron sued over alleged DRAM price fixing amid record memory costs — lawsuit claims coordinated HBM shift was cover to curtail DDR3 and DDR4 production
items.description: Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron were sued on June 25th in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 13 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron were sued on June 25th in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California, where 17 plaintiffs accuse the three memory makers of illegally coordinating to restrict DRAM supply and inflate prices that the complaint says have risen roughly 700% over four years. The class action, filed as Garciaguirre v. Samsung Electronics and assigned to Judge Noel Wise, invokes Section 1 of the Sherman Act and targets companies that together hold around 90% of the global DRAM market. Samsung and SK hynix have pleaded guilty to criminal DRAM price fixing once before, with the latter paying a $185 million fine in April 2005.The complaint argues that the three companies used a coordinated shift toward high-bandwidth memory (HBM), the stacked DRAM that feeds AI accelerators, as a cover to curtail production of older DDR3 and DDR4 modules. That contraction in commodity DRAM, the plaintiffs argue, pushed prices to record highs while no rival could step in: building a new DRAM fab costs tens of billions and takes years, leaving the incumbents free to cut output without fear of being undercut.The named plaintiffs include 14 individuals and three small PC businesses, among them Troy's Computers and Florida repair outfit My Florida PC, and they cite Apple's recent iPad and Mac price increases as evidence of the squeeze. They’re seeking class status, an injunction, and treble damages as a result.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: However, this case revisits a precedent that was already established in the same courthouse. A 2018 class action brought by law firm Hagens Berman made similar claims about parallel production cuts, and the district court dismissed it in 2020. The Ninth Circuit upheld that dismissal in 2022, ruling the trio's conduct was “more likely explained by lawful, unchoreographed free-market behavior” than by an illegal agreement.It was held at the time that the plaintiffs’ eight “plus factors” didn’t clear Section 1’s threshold, which demands evidence of an actual agreement rather than the conscious parallelism common in a three-supplier market. The new complaint leans on the pivot to HBM as the additional evidence that this earlier lawsuit lacked. You may like Samsung and SK hynix warn AI-driven memory shortages could last until 2027 and beyond, as HBM demand explodes Class-action lawsuit filed against HDD suspension assembly makers Team Group agrees to $1.1 million DRAM settlement in another false advertising lawsuit The three memory makers have publicly stated they’re operating independently while redirecting capacity to HBM, and senior executives have warned the shortage could run for years. Investment bank Jefferies expects DRAM prices to rise another 40% to 50% in the third quarter and a further 30% to 40% in the fourth, with no meaningful relief before 2028. The allegations remain unproven, and the defendants have not yet responded in court. items.titleZh: 三星、SK海力士和美光因涉嫌在创纪录内存成本中操纵DRAM价格被起诉——诉讼称协调转向HBM是掩盖削减DDR3和DDR4生产的幌子
items.descriptionZh:
2025年6月25日，三星电子、SK海力士和美光科技在美国加州北区联邦地区法院被提起集体诉讼。17名原告指控这三大DRAM制造商合谋限制DRAM供应并哄抬价格，称相关产品价格在四年间上涨约700%。该诉讼（Garciaguirre诉三星电子案）由法官Noel Wise审理，援引了《谢尔曼法案》第1条，剑指三家合计占据全球DRAM市场约90%份额的企业。值得注意的是，三星和SK海力士曾因DRAM价格操纵刑事案认罪，其中SK海力士于2005年4月被罚款1.85亿美元。

原告的核心论点是，三家厂商利用向高带宽内存（HBM）的集体转向作为掩护——HBM是一种为AI加速器供应的堆叠式DRAM——以此掩盖对老旧DDR3和DDR4模块产量的削减。这种通用DRAM的收缩，原告认为，将价格推至历史高位，而由于新建一座DRAM晶圆厂需要耗费数百亿美元且耗时数年，没有其他竞争对手能够介入，使得现有厂商可以放心削减产量而不必担心被低价竞争。

原告包括14名个人和三家小型PC企业，如Troy's Computers和佛罗里达州的维修公司My Florida PC。他们以苹果公司近期上调iPad和Mac价格为证据，证明这种供应挤压已传导至终端产品。原告方寻求集体诉讼地位、禁令以及三倍损害赔偿。

然而，此案面临此前在同一法院确立的先例。2018年，Hagens Berman律所提起的一起集体诉讼曾提出类似的生产削减共谋指控，但地区法院于2020年驳回，第九巡回上诉法院于2022年维持了驳回决定，裁定三家的行为“更可能是合法的、无预先安排的自由市场行为”而非非法协议。当时法院认为，原告提出的八个“附加因素”未能达到《谢尔曼法案》第1条的证明门槛——该条款要求证明存在实际协议，而非三个供应商市场中常见的“有意识并行行为”。此次新的诉讼将转向HBM作为上一案件缺乏的额外证据。

三大内存厂商公开表示，它们各自独立运营，同时将产能转向HBM，其高管还警告称，短缺可能持续数年。投行杰富瑞（Jefferies）预计，DRAM价格将在第三季度再涨40%至50%，第四季度再涨30%至40%，2028年前不会出现实质性缓解。目前相关指控尚未得到证实，被告方尚未在法庭上作出回应。

items.title: Imec's 2026 roadmap details 0.3nm nodes by 2038, CFET transistors become viable at 0.7nm — company redefines Moore's Law as cell sizes gain importance for density
items.description: As CPP shrinking stalls, chipmakers find a new way to increase transistor density. Imec foresees 0.3nm in 2038, CFET insertion in 2038, HLSI era.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Imec's semiconductor process technology roadmap sets the general direction of industry development and showcases the challenges the sector is set to face over the next few decades. The roadmap gives us an idea of the timelines for the next major process nodes and transistor architectures the company will research and develop in cooperation with industry giants, such as TSMC, Intel, Nvidia, AMD, Samsung, and ASML, among many others.Imec's latest production node roadmap shows that the international research and development organization envisions 3 angstrom-class (0.3nm) fabrication technologies by 2038, but expects contact poly pitch (CPP) to stop scaling at A10 in 2030. While things might not be looking great for Moore's Law for imec, to continue scaling the chipmaker will need to adopt new technologies, such as CFET transistors and likely Hyper-NA EUV Lithography systems.GAA transistors have seven years leftAs the production of semiconductors becomes substantially more complicated, chipmakers no longer introduce all-new process technologies every couple of years. Instead, they typically roll out a new node generation every three years, with annual incremental enhancements in between. TSMC ramped N3B production in 2023, then followed it up with N3E in 2024, and N3P in 2025. Intel planned to follow the same pattern with 20A in 2024 (which was canceled), 18A in 2025, and 18A-P in 2027.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Next-generation process technologies will continue to emerge at similar cadences, according to imec's roadmap. (Image credit: Imec)Interuniversity Microelectronics Centre considers that we now live in the 2nm-class era (N2) with contact poly pitch (CPP) of around 48nm, as well as cell height of around 132nm and 6 metal tracks. The reality may be a bit different as Intel's 18A has a CPP of 50nm as well as a cell height of 160nm (high density) or 190nm (high performance), whereas TSMC's N3 can boast with a CPP of 45nm. N2 (or 18A, if you wish) will be followed by its performance and efficiency-enhanced version in the next couple of years, which is in line with how the industry has been operating in recent years. You may like IBM goes sub-1nm, develops 0.7nm-class technology Post-silicon era gets closer as industry giants crack the 2D transistor scaling bottleneck with breakthrough tech Leading-edge foundry roadmaps for TSMC, Intel and Samsung — outlining the path to 1.4nm nodes and beyond "Of course, we are going to extend our logic roadmap to the next generation beyond N2," said Julien Ryckaert, vice president of R&D at imec. "As you know, in two nanometers we have already jumped into a new technology device paradigm in the nanosheet era, and that is going to bring us deep into the Angstrom node." (Image credit: Imec)Imec expects the A14-class to emerge in 2028. TSMC expects to start high-volume manufacturing using A14 in late 2028, so the actual ramp will happen in 2029. Intel's 14A follows the same pattern. With A14, imec projects CPP to shrink to 45nm and cell height to drop to 115nm and 5.5 metal tracks. Around 2030–2031, imec expects an A10-class technology — or a 1nm-class — with a 42 nm CPP and 98 nm cell height, which will still rely on a 5.5-track architecture.It is noteworthy that gate-all-around (GAA) transistor-based nodes can be implemented either with conventional frontside power delivery networks or with backside power delivery, which reflects both imec's and TSMC's belief that BSPDN will not immediately become mandatory across all applications, as many of them do not benefit from it.It is also worth mentioning that imec expects High-NA EUV tools insertion at A14, which is in line with Intel's plans, but not with TSMC's plans.CFET insertion in the early 2030sThe roadmap becomes particularly interesting at the A7 generation, which imec expects to come in 2033. While CPP remains at 42 nm, cell height drops to roughly 80nm, and the standard-cell architecture moves to 4.5 tracks. More importantly, A7 is the point where CFET emerges as a serious candidate for production insertion. Instead of placing n-type and p-type transistors side by side, CFET stacks them vertically, which adds a third dimension to transistor scaling. What to read next TSMC's details next-gen CoWoS roadmap: over 14-reticle packages and 48x leap in compute power expected by 2029 Imec builds world's first High-NA EUV-fabricated quantum dot qubit device Analyzing TSMC's fab expansion roadmap — multi-fab N2 ramp, CoWoS, SoIC, and uncorking bottlenecks Imec’s roadmap explicitly positions CFET as the leading contender for A7, which means that the organization sees conventional nanosheet architectures approaching practical scaling limits in the early 2030s. Yet, since A7's CPP does not change from A10, chipmakers may or may not adopt the all-new transistor architecture at A7. Also note that imec seems to consider BSPDN as mandatory for CFETs. (Image credit: Imec)"Moving into A7, the seventh-angstrom generation, which is the fourth generation of nanosheet, we see more and more challenges in scaling the conventional nanosheet device technology," Ryckaert said. "There is a contender that we have already mentioned as well in previous presentations, where CFET could start emerging as the solution for the next era of transistors."Beyond A7, the roadmap seems to depend on CFET evolution. The A5 generation, expected in 2035–2036, retains a 42nm CPP but reduces cell height to about 64nm using a 4-track library. By 2038, the roadmap reaches A3 with a 39nm CPP and 50nm cell height. At this point, imec envisions sequential CFET implementations and eventually bonded CFET structures that further exploit vertical integration. In fact, vertical integration seems to be the new way we should look at Moore's Law's evolution. Meanwhile, to get to a 39nm CPP and 50nm cell height, chipmakers might need to use Hyper-NA EUV lithography scanners, according to imec.Redefining Moore's Law (Image credit: Imec)The most interesting thing about imec's roadmap is that it essentially redefines what Moore's Law means. Traditionally, we consider Moore's Law as the observation that the number of transistors on a chip of a certain size doubles every 18 – 24 months, as they are getting smaller.The fact that imec shows CPP stuck at 42 nm from A10 through A5 is almost an admission that classical transistor scaling is running out of steam, and future density gains must come from vertical integration. In the imec roadmap, transistors are still getting denser, but not exactly because individual transistors are shrinking at the same pace they used to decades ago, but because chip designers can fit more logic gates into a given area because of different transistor architectures, 3D integration, or backside power delivery.As a result, in the coming years, we may no longer care how many nanometers a gate pitch is, or individual transistors, but rather the size of a standard cell. After all, when companies like AMD, Intel, or Nvidia design a chip, they do not place individual transistors, but actual blocks built from standard cells. Yet, calculating the size of a standard cell is complicated because while cell height is fixed, its width is not, and depends on the actual function.Library height × CPP is not the size of a specific standard cell. It is the fundamental footprint unit of a standard-cell library and a widely used proxy for logic density. Actual standard cells have that height, but their width varies depending on function. Instead, the industry uses such metrics as logic cell area (standard-cell footprint) — Cell Height × CPP — that measures the actual footprint of the logic building blocks that designers use, not just the dimensions of individual transistors.The transition from 6-track cells at N2 to 3-track cells at A3 illustrates how future density gains will rely as much on shrinking standard-cell height as on reducing transistor pitch. As a result, despite the fact that CPP shrinkage is expected to stall for years, logic cell area is set to decrease; designers will be able to extract transistor density gain from future nodes, proving that Moore's Law is still here.Hetrogenous Large-Scale Integration × Cross-Technology Co-OptimizationGiven all the changes that the semiconductor industry is already experiencing and what is set to come, imec believes the sector is entering a new era that it calls Heterogeneous Large-Scale Integration (HLSI). The concept reflects a shift away from traditional VLSI scaling, where progress largely depended on the evolution of transistors and increasing transistor density, toward a model that combines multiple technologies within a single compute platform.Future systems will rely on heterogeneous integration of logic, memory, power-delivery circuitry, and optical I/O using advanced 3D and 3D + 2.5D packaging technologies, according to imec's predictions. Of course, the organization expects AI workloads to become the main driver of semiconductor demand, so expect both compute architectures and the semiconductor industry to evolve in a direction that satisfies the needs of AI applications."As we will move deeper into AI-driven architecture, we will need to double down on the heterogeneity that technology offers, and this will probably move the VLSI paradigm to the HLSI paradigm, the Heterogeneous Large Scale Integration," Ryckaert said.To optimize future platforms on the system level rather than develop individual components in isolation, imec has established its Cross-Technology Co-Optimization (XTCO) framework, which could be seen as an integral part of the HLSI vision. XTCO is designed to wed development logic, memory, interconnects, power delivery, cooling, and packaging, and assesses their impact on key system metrics such as compute density, energy efficiency, thermal performance, and memory.It remains to be seen how this is going to work out, if at all, given the fact that logic process technologies are developed at foundries, memory technologies are designed at DRAM makers, whereas cooling is developed at third parties like CoolIt or Frore Systems.Power and coolingAs individual chips get denser and more power-hungry, power delivery is set to become a critical bottleneck, which is why all leading chipmakers — Intel, Samsung, and TSMC — are implementing or set to implement backside power delivery technologies and integrated voltage regulators.Imec expects future AI accelerators and CPUs to rely on a combination of BSPDN, IVRs, embedded capacitors, and advanced power semiconductors to reduce losses and improve efficiency. Over time, more power-conversion stages are expected to migrate from racks and motherboards into packages themselves to deliver cleaner power directly to transistors.Since we are talking about multi-chiplet packages consuming kilowatts of power, the importance of cooling is hard to overestimate. For sure, 3D stacking and CFETs will not make cooling any easier because thermal power density is set to increase linearly with the number of transistors, thermal resistance is set to increase, and local hotspots will become an even bigger problem than they are today. As a result, imec expects future compute platforms to rely on a combination of more advanced cooling technologies, improved heat spreading, fine-grained thermal sensors, and system-level thermal optimization techniques. "At the end of the day, what we need to achieve is a reduced energy cost of data movement. We need to improve the TDP for better thermal management," Ryckaert said. "We need to improve the efficiency of the power delivery, and we need to obviously increase the compute density to improve the functionality."In short, useful future scaling will depend not only on the ability to build transistors and increase their density, but on delivering power efficiently and removing heat effectively.Paving the path forwardImec's latest semiconductor roadmap projects logic process technologies all the way to A3 generation around 2038 and argues that Moore's Law can continue despite the slowing pace of traditional transistor scaling. While the Dennard scaling for semiconductors is over, there are plenty of interesting things incoming.According to the roadmap, conventional gate-all-around nanosheet transistors should remain viable through A10, while CFET architectures become a candidate for production insertion at the A7 generation around 2033. Meanwhile, future transistor density gains are expected to come from vertical integration, reduced standard-cell footprints, and eventually sequential and bonded CFET structures rather than from aggressive shrinking of transistor dimensions.
items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: Imec 2026年路线图：到2038年实现0.3nm节点，0.7nm时CFET晶体管可行——公司重新定义摩尔定律，单元尺寸对密度愈发重要
items.descriptionZh:
imec（比利时微电子研究中心）发布了最新的半导体工艺技术路线图，为整个行业未来数十年的发展指明了方向，也揭示了芯片制造将面临的严峻挑战。这份路线图由imec与台积电、英特尔、英伟达、AMD、三星、ASML等业界巨头合作研发，明确了未来主要工艺节点和晶体管架构的时间表。

**路线图概览：从2nm到3埃米时代**

imec认为我们目前正处于2nm级（N2）时代，其接触多晶硅间距（CPP）约为48nm，单元高度约为132nm，采用6个金属轨道。但实际参数因厂商而异：英特尔18A工艺的CPP为50nm，单元高度为160nm（高密度）或190nm（高性能）；台积电N3工艺的CPP为45nm。

imec预计，**A14（14埃米级）将于2028年问世**。台积电计划在2028年底开始A14的高量产，真正爬坡则在2029年；英特尔14A也遵循相同节奏。A14的CPP将缩至45nm，单元高度降至115nm，采用5.5个金属轨道。

**到2030-2031年，A10（1nm级）将登场**，CPP为42nm，单元高度98nm，仍采用5.5轨道架构。值得注意的是，基于GAA（全环绕栅极）晶体管的节点既可采用传统正面供电网络，也可采用背面供电网络（BSPDN）。imec和台积电均认为BSPDN不会立即在所有应用中成为强制要求，因为许多应用并不能从中受益。另外，imec预计High-NA EUV光刻工具将在A14节点引入，这与英特尔计划一致，但台积电尚未跟进。

**CFET晶体管：2030年代初的正式引入**

路线图最引人注目的部分是**A7（7埃米级，预计2033年）**。虽然CPP仍维持42nm，但单元高度将骤降至约80nm，标准单元架构变为4.5个轨道。更重要的是，A7是**CFET（互补场效应晶体管）作为量产候选方案**的起点。与传统将n型和p型晶体管并排放置不同，CFET将它们垂直堆叠，从而增加晶体管缩放的第三维度。imec明确将CFET定位为A7的主要候选，这意味着传统的纳米片架构在2030年代初将接近实际缩放极限。由于A7的CPP与A10相同，芯片制造商也可能选择不采用全新晶体管架构。imec认为BSPDN对于CFET是强制性的。

**A5（预计2035-2036年）** 和随后**A3（预计2038年）** 的演进依赖于CFET的发展。A5仍保持42nm CPP，但单元高度降至约64nm，采用4轨道库。到A3，CPP缩减至39nm，单元高度降至50nm。此时，imec设想了顺序式CFET实现以及最终的键合式CFET结构，进一步利用垂直集成。同时，要实现39nm CPP和50nm单元高度，芯片制造商可能需要使用**Hyper-NA EUV光刻设备**。

**摩尔定律的重新定义**

imec路线图最有趣的一点是它实际上重新定义了摩尔定律。传统上，摩尔定律是指芯片上晶体管数量每18-24个月翻一番。但imec显示从A10到A5，CPP将卡在42nm，这几乎等于承认传统晶体管缩放正在失去动力，未来的密度增长必须来自垂直集成。在imec路线图中，晶体管密度仍在增加，但并非因为单个晶体管尺寸以过去同样的速度缩小，而是因为不同的晶体管架构、3D集成或背面供电，使得芯片设计人员能在给定面积内塞入更多逻辑门。

因此，未来我们可能不再关注栅极间距或单个晶体管的纳米数，而是关注标准单元的尺寸。从N2的6轨道单元到A3的3轨道单元，这种转变说明了未来的密度增长将同样依赖于缩小标准单元高度和减小晶体管间距。尽管CPP缩放在几年内停滞，逻辑单元面积仍将减小，设计人员仍能从未来节点中获得晶体管密度增益，证明摩尔定律仍在延续。

**异构大规模集成（HLSI）与跨技术协同优化（XTCO）**

面对这些变化，imec认为行业正在进入一个称为**异构大规模集成（HLSI）**的新时代。这个概念反映了从传统VLSI缩放（主要依赖晶体管演进和密度增加）向在一个计算平台内融合多种技术的模型转变。未来系统将依赖于逻辑、存储器、供电电路和光学I/O的异构集成，使用先进的3D和3D+2.5D封装技术。人工智能工作负载将成为半导体需求的主要驱动力，计算架构和半导体行业都将朝着满足AI应用需求的方向发展。

为了在系统层面优化未来平台，imec建立了**跨技术协同优化（XTCO）**框架。该框架旨在将逻辑、存储器、互连、供电、冷却和封装的开发结合起来，评估它们对计算密度、能效、热性能和内存等关键系统指标的影响。然而，由于逻辑工艺由代工厂开发、存储器由DRAM制造商设计、冷却由第三方公司开发，这一框架如何实际运作仍有待观察。

**供电与散热的挑战**

随着单个芯片越来越密集、功耗越来越高，供电成为关键瓶颈。这就是所有领先芯片制造商（英特尔、三星、台积电）都在实施或计划实施背面供电技术和集成稳压器（IVR）的原因。imec预计未来的AI加速器和CPU将依赖BSPDN、IVR、嵌入式电容器和先进功率半导体的组合来降低损耗、提高效率。越来越多的电源转换阶段将从机架和主板转移到封装内部，以便将更清洁的电力直接输送到晶体管。

由于我们正在谈论功耗达到千瓦级的多芯片封装，散热的重要性怎么强调也不为过。3D堆叠和CFET并不会使散热更容易，因为热功率密度将随晶体管数量线性增加，热阻会增加，局部热点问题将比今天更严重。因此，imec预计未来的计算平台将结合更先进的冷却技术、改进的散热技术、精细温度传感器和系统级热优化技术。最终目标是要降低数据移动的能耗成本，改善热管理，提高供电效率，并增加计算密度以提升功能。

**总结：前行的道路**

imec的最新半导体路线图将逻辑工艺技术展望到2038年的A3代，并论证了尽管传统晶体管缩放速度放缓，摩尔定律仍可延续。虽然半导体的登纳德缩放已经结束，但仍有大量值得期待的新技术涌现。根据路线图，传统的GAA纳米片晶体管将在A10节点之前保持可行，而CFET架构将在2033年的A7节点成为量产候选。未来的晶体管密度提升将主要来自垂直集成、减小的标准单元面积，以及最终顺序堆叠和键合式CFET结构，而非激进的晶体管尺寸缩小。整个行业正从单纯的晶体管缩放转向包含供电、散热、异构集成的系统级优化，以支撑AI时代的计算需求。

items.title: Open Models, Closed Environments: Palantir Brings Secure AI to US Agencies With NVIDIA Nemotron
items.description: Showcasing the importance of open source innovation in American AI, Palantir’s new intelligent engine — introduced today — uses NVIDIA Nemotron open models to serve the needs of U.S. government agencies. Open source software has long been a pillar of U.S. technology leadership.  In 1969, DARPA connected four university computers — from UCLA, Stanford, UCSB […]
items.content: Showcasing the importance of open source innovation in American AI, Palantir’s new intelligent engine — introduced today — uses NVIDIA Nemotron open models to serve the needs of U.S. government agencies. Open source software has long been a pillar of U.S. technology leadership. In 1969, DARPA connected four university computers — from UCLA, Stanford, UCSB and the University of Utah — laying the infrastructure backbone that became the internet. In those early days, U.S.-led open source contributions also drove leadership in coding languages, with UNIX in 1969 and C at Bell Labs in 1972. These languages led to more open source software building on those foundations, including the Linux Kernel in 1991, GitHub in 2008 and Docker in 2013. Today, open models are making frontier-level AI broadly accessible, with control over customization and trust through transparency. They give enterprises and government agencies the ability to inspect, adapt and deploy AI in sensitive environments, making them essential for national security, corporate sustainability and industrial innovation. With domain-optimized harnesses, strong open models can deliver frontier capabilities while helping customers retain control over proprietary data, model weights and deployment environments. Today’s Palantir announcement brings NVIDIA Nemotron open models into air-gapped environments — secure setups that are completely isolated from unsecured networks — on NVIDIA accelerated computing. Palantir will use NVIDIA Nemotron open models to build custom frontier-quality models to serve the U.S. government. Many of the government’s operations mirror private-sector enterprises — including commerce, energy, healthcare, agriculture, education and transportation. With about 3 million civilian employees, the U.S. government is essentially one of the world’s largest enterprises. Providing critical services across so many disciplines is incredibly complex. AI can help streamline this complexity and boost insights to drive productivity. From food safety to maintaining safety on interstate highway infrastructure, AI can help government agencies tackle operational challenges, just like American public sector businesses. With this new engine, agencies and operators can run customized Nemotron models on their own infrastructure, train on their own data and retain full ownership of the resulting models — including the weights that encode their operational knowledge. Palantir’s Sovereign AI Operating System — built on AIP, Ontology, Foundry and Apollo — handles the operational and data authorization layer for easy deployment in sensitive environments. Explicit data authorization, architecturally enforced isolation and full auditability are already central to Palantir’s Sovereign AI Operating System. As these customized models are used in production, agencies and operators can continually improve them within their own environments using new data and feedback. This creates a data flywheel that continually optimizes model performance while keeping data, models and auditability under customer control. While NVIDIA Nemotron open models provide a customizable and continually learning model layer on Palantir’s Sovereign AI Operating System, enterprise-grade deployments can be supported through the NVIDIA AI Enterprise software suite. Open Models Support Trust, Access, Control and Lower Costs Together, NVIDIA Nemotron open models and Palantir’s critical infrastructure products enable trust, accessibility, control and lower costs. Trust through transparency: Through independent review of open models, researchers can identify vulnerabilities, biases and unintended behaviors that a single organization may miss. This visibility then allows for models to be refined to address these issues, improving safety through transparency. Customization and control: Companies, governments and developers can modify and fine-tune open models to better suit their use cases. Open models can be deployed in regulated environments, like in the financial industry, where closed models might breach data security or other privacy laws. Lower costs fuel economic development: Open models are in broad use, with about two-thirds of companies already using them and reporting on their cost efficiency. Organizations note that these cost savings are an important factor as they build AI that can scale with success. The combination of NVIDIA Nemotron open models with Palantir’s critical infrastructure products — AIP, Foundry, Ontology and Apollo — bolsters U.S. technology leadership for government agencies and commercial businesses alike. Running the models on air-gapped NVIDIA-powered infrastructure keeps the data and models secure — and ready to support the most important missions. Learn more about NVIDIA Nemotron and the Palantir Sovereign AI Operating System Reference Architecture with NVIDIA.
items.author: Justin Boitano
items.titleZh: Open Models, Closed Environments: Palantir Brings Secure AI to US Agencies With NVIDIA Nemotron
items.descriptionZh:
Palantir 公司近期推出了一款基于 NVIDIA Nemotron 开放模型的全新智能引擎，旨在为美国政府机构提供先进的人工智能服务。这一举措凸显了开源创新在美国人工智能领域的重要地位，开放模型正成为国家技术领导力的关键组成部分。

开源软件长期以来都是美国技术领先的基石。1969年，DARPA将加州大学洛杉矶分校、斯坦福大学、加州大学圣巴巴拉分校和犹他大学的四台大学计算机连接起来，奠定了后来互联网的基础架构。早期，美国主导的开源贡献还推动了编程语言的突破，例如1969年的UNIX和1972年贝尔实验室开发的C语言。这些语言催生了更多开源软件，如1991年的Linux内核、2008年的GitHub和2013年的Docker。如今，开放模型让前沿AI变得广泛可及，通过透明性实现定制化控制和信任，使企业和政府机构能够在敏感环境中审查、调整和部署AI，这对国家安全、企业可持续性和工业创新至关重要。借助领域优化的框架，强大的开放模型能够提供前沿能力，同时帮助客户保留对专有数据、模型权重和部署环境的控制。

Palantir 此次宣布将 NVIDIA Nemotron 开放模型引入气隙环境——一种与不安全的网络完全隔离的安全设置——并运行在 NVIDIA 加速计算平台上。Palantir 将使用 NVIDIA Nemotron 开放模型构建定制的、达到前沿质量水平的AI模型，专门服务于美国政府。美国政府许多运作与私营企业相似，涵盖商业、能源、医疗、农业、教育和交通等领域。拥有约300万文职雇员的美国政府实际上是世界上最大的企业之一。在如此多的领域提供关键服务极为复杂，AI有助于简化这种复杂性并提升洞察力以实现生产力增长。从食品安全到州际高速公路安全维护，AI可以帮助政府机构应对运营挑战，正如美国公共部门企业所经历的那样。

通过这款新引擎，政府机构及其操作人员可以在自己的基础设施上运行定制的 Nemotron 模型，用自己的数据训练模型，并完全拥有最终模型的所有权——包括编码了其运营知识的权重。Palantir 的主权AI操作系统基于 AIP、Ontology、Foundry 和 Apollo 构建，负责运营和数据授权层，便于在敏感环境中部署。显式数据授权、架构性隔离以及完全可审计性已经是 Palantir 主权AI操作系统的核心特性。当这些定制模型投入生产后，机构可以利用新数据和反馈在其内部环境中持续改进模型，从而形成一个数据飞轮，不断优化模型性能，同时保持数据、模型和审计性完全在客户控制之下。NVIDIA Nemotron 开放模型提供了可定制且持续学习的模型层，部署在 Palantir 的主权AI操作系统上，而企业级部署可以通过 NVIDIA AI Enterprise 软件套件获得支持。

开放模型支持信任、可访问性、控制和低成本。NVIDIA Nemotron 开放模型与 Palantir 的关键基础设施产品相结合，能够实现信任、可访问性、控制和更低成本。信任来源于透明性：通过独立审查开放模型，研究人员可以发现单一组织可能忽略的漏洞、偏见和意外行为，这种可见性使得模型得以优化以解决这些问题，从而通过透明性提高安全性。定制与控制：企业、政府和开发者可以修改和微调开放模型以更好地适应其使用场景，开放模型可以在受监管的环境（如金融行业）中部署，而封闭模型可能违反数据安全或隐私法律。低成本推动经济发展：开放模型被广泛使用，约三分之二的企业已经使用它们并报告了成本效益。组织指出，这些成本节省是构建可扩展AI的重要因素。

NVIDIA Nemotron 开放模型与 Palantir 的关键基础设施产品（AIP、Foundry、Ontology 和 Apollo）的组合，增强了美国在政府机构和商业企业中的技术领导力。在基于NVIDIA的气隙基础设施上运行模型，确保了数据和模型的安全性，并随时准备支持最重要的任务。了解更多关于 NVIDIA Nemotron 和 Palantir 主权AI操作系统参考架构的信息，请访问相关资源。

history.titleZh: 开源RISC-V平台从RTL到芯片培训芯片设计师（苏黎世联邦理工学院、lowRISC、博洛尼亚大学）
history.descriptionZh:
瑞士苏黎世联邦理工学院（ETH Zurich）、lowRISC 和博洛尼亚大学（University of Bologna）的研究人员联合发表了一篇题为《Croc：基于领域专用全流程开源硅芯片培养下一代芯片设计师》的技术论文，提出了一套名为 **Croc** 的开源、可定制 RISC-V SoC 平台及配套教学流程。该平台的核心目标是让芯片设计学生能够以“领域专用”（Domain-Specific）的方式，完整经历从架构设计、RTL 开发到物理设计（Physical Design）乃至最终流片的全链条实践，从而填补传统芯片设计教育中理论与实际流片经验之间的鸿沟。

**背景与上下文**  
随着摩尔定律放缓，领域专用架构（如 AI 加速器、IoT 处理器）成为提升性能与能效的关键。然而，芯片设计教育长期面临两大痛点：一是商业 EDA 工具成本高昂且封闭，学生难以接触完整的设计流程；二是从 RTL 到 GDS（最终版图）的物理设计环节往往被简化为理论讲授，缺乏真实的流片实践。Croc 应运而生，它基于开放指令集 RISC-V，利用完全开源的 EDA 工具链（如 OpenROAD、Yosys、Verilator 等），让学生能在低成本甚至零成本前提下体验从架构探索到芯片生成的完整路径。

**核心技术原理与创新点**  
Croc 的核心是一个专为教学优化的 RISC-V 内核——**Croc Core**，它是一个 32 位、支持 RV32IMC 指令集（含乘除法和压缩指令）的单发射微控制器级处理器。关键创新包括：  
1. **领域专用定制化**：Croc 平台设计为“可组合”——学生可以围绕 Croc Core 添加自定义协处理器、加速器或外设（如用于神经网络推理的 MAC 单元、信号处理单元等），并以 SoC 形式集成。这鼓励学生在架构层面思考专用化设计。  
2. **端到端开源流程**：教学流程覆盖架构级性能评估（使用 Spike/riscv-tests）、RTL 设计（SystemVerilog）、逻辑综合（Yosys）、物理设计（OpenROAD）以及最终生成 GDSII 版图。所有工具均为开源，且配置了教学友好的脚本和文档，降低学习曲线。  
3. **面向流片的设计约束**：Croc 专门针对 180nm 或 130nm 等成熟工艺节点（如 SkyWater 130nm 开源 PDK）进行优化，该节点成本低、设计规则简单，适合教学流片。学生能实际将自己设计的芯片送到代工厂（如通过 Efabless 或 MPW 项目），获得真实芯片。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
论文公开了 Croc Core 的部分指标（基于 SkyWater 130nm 工艺）：  
- 面积：约 0.1 mm²（不含缓存和协处理器）  
- 最大运行频率：约 50 MHz（在典型电压下）  
- 功耗：数 mW 量级  
- 性能：约 1.5 DMIPS/MHz，与同类开源内核（如 PicoRV32）相当，但拥有更灵活的 SoC 总线接口（支持 AXI4-Lite 或 TileLink）和更低面积成本。  

与商业教学 SoC（如 ARM Cortex-M0）相比，Croc 仅需 1/10 甚至更低的流片成本，且学生能完全透明地查看和修改所有 RTL 及物理设计文件。论文还展示了多个教学案例：一个添加了 AES 硬件加速器的同学完成的设计，在面积仅增加 15% 的情况下，AES-128 加密速度提升约 20 倍。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
1. **教育领域**：Croc 提供了一种可复用的开源课程模板，适合研究生级高级数字设计、VLSI 或计算机体系结构课程。学生可以在一个学期内完成从零到流片的完整项目，真正理解设计决策对性能、功耗、面积的物理影响。  
2. **科研与原型验证**：由于开源和可定制，研究人员可以基于 Croc 快速验证新的领域专用架构（如边缘 AI 加速器或传感器融合处理器），并低成本流片获取实测数据，避免依赖模拟器的不确定性。  
3. **开源硬件生态**：Croc 与 RISC-V 社区紧密结合，其 SoC 设计可被其他开源项目重用（如作为 IoT 节点控制器的基带芯片），进一步推动“开源硅”的民主化。  

总之，Croc 不仅是一个教学工具，更是一个弥合芯片设计教育与工业实践之间的桥梁。它通过完全开源、领域专用、端到端可流片的路径，让下一代芯片设计师不仅掌握理论，更拥有亲手制造硅芯片的真实经验，这对于应对未来多样化、专用化芯片的需求至关重要。

history.title: Legacy Nvidia RTX 3060 12GB returns to retail five years after original launch, priced at $339 — resurrected GPU strategy that Jensen called a 'good idea' apparently comes to fruition
history.description: After Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said that "it's a good idea" to consider re-introducing older GPUs made on trailing process nodes, the five-year-old RTX 3060 is back on e-tailer shelves, priced at $339.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 6 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Back at CES 2026, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said that "it's a good idea" to consider reintroducing older GeForce RTX graphics cards with GPUs from trailing-edge nodes to alleviate pricing and availability concerns around cutting-edge RTX 50-series products. It appears that the idea may now have become reality. After a hiatus that began late last year, RTX 3060 12GB cards (first launched in early 2021) are beginning to reappear as new stock at e-tailers.We've spotted a Gigabyte RTX 3060 12GB card available direct from new stock at Newegg for $339.99, or just $10 above its MSRP from over five years ago. This Windforce card is a straightforward dual-fan model with no frills, but tellingly, it carries a rare Rev2.0 suffix, directly indicating a revision or new model of some kind. That revision number aside, it's identical (as far as we can tell) to the Rev1.0 card that presumably appeared nearer to the RTX 3060 12GB's launch.It appears that different board partners are working to quietly bring back the RTX 3060 12GB worldwide, as ComputerBase notes the availability of an Asus Dual card in the European market. We wouldn't be surprised if that product makes its way Stateside, as well.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Gamers may have hoped that the reintroduction of an older product from a foundry and a process node that isn't capacity-constrained (namely Samsung 8nm), as well as an older memory technology, would have resulted in some pricing relief, but that doesn't appear to be the case.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)The RTX 3060 12GB is reappearing for just a few bucks less than the far superior RTX 5060, which offers both stronger baseline performance and unfettered performance with the latest DLSS 4.5 upscaling tech. Heck, even the entry-level RTX 5050 outpaces the RTX 3060 in our most recent tests.The 3060's extra 4GB of VRAM compared to those products only comes into play with settings and resolutions where its shader horsepower is already inadequate to produce playable frame rates, so it's more of a psychological advantage than a practical one.So why this product and why now? As AI wafer demand on cutting-edge nodes only grows, it's possible that simple economics dictate that the opportunity cost of producing entry-level Blackwell GPUs for the desktop is simply too great compared to the revenue and margin opportunities of making data center Rubin GPUs for the countless Vera Rubin NVL72 racks that the company is doubtless planning to ship later this year.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIn the event that Nvidia does prioritize datacenter products over consumer ones, the already elevated prices of entry-level RTX 50-series cards might give way to a situation where one simply can't buy these lower-priced, lower-margin products at any cost. And in that event, the aging RTX 3060 could be one of the few sub-$350 options available to gamers who need to build a new system or upgrade from an aging graphics card.And frankly, that's a poor outcome for gamers, as the RTX 3060 can't run the latest DLSS 4.5 upscaling model at its full potential due to the lack of proper FP8 acceleration on older Turing and Ampere GPUs. And it can't run DLSS Frame Generation at all, leaving gamers to deal with cross-platform solutions like FSR and XeSS frame generation from other vendors. Those technologies are incredibly useful tools in getting the best experience out of modern games, and if they're only available on a smaller subset of more expensive GPUs, that further stratifies the already stratified modern PC gaming experience.We've reached out to Gigabyte and Nvidia for comment on the reappearance of the RTX 3060 and will report what we learn if we hear back. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Jeffrey Kampman
history.titleZh: 经典Nvidia RTX 3060 12GB在首次推出五年后重返零售市场，售价339美元——黄仁勋曾称为“好主意”的复活GPU策略显然已取得成果
history.descriptionZh:
在CES 2026大会上，英伟达CEO黄仁勋曾表示，考虑重新推出基于较落后制程节点的旧款GeForce RTX显卡，以缓解RTX 50系列显卡因尖端制程产能紧张带来的价格和供应问题。如今这一想法似乎已成为现实：在去年底短暂停产后，RTX 3060 12GB显卡（最初于2021年初发布）正以新库存的形式重新出现在零售商货架上。

我们在Newegg上发现了一款技嘉RTX 3060 12GB Windforce双风扇显卡，售价339.99美元，仅比五年前的建议零售价高出10美元。值得注意的是，该卡带有罕见的Rev2.0后缀，直接表明这是一款修订版或新型号。除版本号外，它与接近首发时出现的Rev1.0版本基本一致。此外，ComputerBase报道称，欧洲市场已出现华硕Dual版本，预计该产品也将进入美国市场。

深入分析技术层面，这款复刻显卡基于三星8nm工艺（非容量受限的尖端节点），并沿用较旧的内存技术。理论上这应能带来价格优势，但现实并非如此——其售价仅比性能远胜于它的RTX 5060低几美元。RTX 5060不仅拥有更强的基线性能，还完整支持最新的DLSS 4.5超分辨率技术；即便是入门级的RTX 5050，在最新测试中也已超越RTX 3060。RTX 3060额外的4GB显存优势，仅在设置和分辨率已使着色器算力无法提供可玩帧率时才能体现，更多是心理上的慰藉而非实际收益。

为何此时复活这款老产品？根本原因在于尖端制程上AI晶圆需求持续攀升。对英伟达而言，生产入门级Blackwell架构桌面GPU的机会成本过高，远不及将产能用于数据中心Rubin GPU（服务于无数Vera Rubin NVL72机架）所带来的收入和利润率。若英伟达真将数据中心产品优先级置于消费级之上，本就昂贵的RTX 50系列入门卡可能面临“有钱也买不到”的局面。此时，老旧的RTX 3060将成为少数售价低于350美元的选项，满足玩家组装新机或升级老显卡的需求。

然而，这对玩家而言是个糟糕的结果。RTX 3060因缺乏对老图灵和安培架构上FP8加速的原生支持，无法以完整潜力运行最新的DLSS 4.5模型，更无法运行DLSS帧生成功能。玩家只能依赖跨平台方案如FSR和XeSS帧生成。这些技术是充分发挥现代游戏体验的关键工具，若仅限少数更昂贵的GPU使用，将进一步加剧本已分层的PC游戏体验。我们已联系技嘉和英伟达就RTX 3060重新上市一事置评，待收到回复后将进一步报道。

history.title: Claude Meets Blackwell Ultra: Anthropic’s Models Now Run on NVIDIA GB300 in Azure
history.description: Anthropic’s Claude models in Microsoft Foundry — hosted on Microsoft Azure and running on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs — are now generally available, giving Azure-native enterprises a powerful new way to build autonomous and domain-specific AI agents. As agentic AI continues to drive enterprise innovation and becomes more autonomous, organizations need access to computing […]
history.content: Anthropic’s Claude models in Microsoft Foundry — hosted on Microsoft Azure and running on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs — are now generally available, giving Azure-native enterprises a powerful new way to build autonomous and domain-specific AI agents. As agentic AI continues to drive enterprise innovation and becomes more autonomous, organizations need access to computing power to build and deploy specialized agents to accelerate essential business tasks. And having great inference performance and efficiency reduces total cost of ownership and drives positive company results. With Claude in Foundry running on NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 systems with NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand networking, enterprises can now build and run more powerful agentic systems, including autonomous and specialized sub-agents that can work across business domains to perform advanced tasks. A Growing Partnership NVIDIA is working with Anthropic to extend developer capabilities by integrating NVIDIA tools into the Anthropic stack. That integration enables enterprises to give Claude agents domain-specific abilities. Through NVIDIA verified agent skills, enabled by access to NVIDIA accelerated computing, enterprises can embed AI agents deeply into their business and use them as the operating system for the organization. Enterprises can run Claude agents on Azure by using the NVIDIA Secure Agent Workspace Reference Design. It provides a blueprint for running autonomous agents in a governed environment where identity, network access, credentials and runtime policy are controlled at the infrastructure level. Claude in Microsoft Foundry accelerated by NVIDIA GB300 GPUs on Azure builds on the strategic partnership Microsoft, NVIDIA and Anthropic announced in November to expand enterprise access to Claude and offer Anthropic models on NVIDIA accelerated computing. Get started by visiting Claude in Microsoft Foundry and learn more in Foundry documentation.
history.author: Dave Salvator
history.titleZh: Claude 遇见 Blackwell Ultra：Anthropic 模型现已可在 Azure 上的 NVIDIA GB300 运行
history.descriptionZh:
Anthropic 的 Claude 模型现已在 Microsoft Foundry 中正式全面可用，该服务托管于 Microsoft Azure 之上，并运行于 NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPU。这为原生使用 Azure 的企业提供了一种强大的新方式，以构建自主且面向特定领域的 AI 智能体。随着智能体 AI 持续推动企业创新并变得更加自主，企业需要获取强大的计算能力来构建和部署专用智能体，从而加速关键业务任务。卓越的推理性能和效率能够降低总拥有成本，并推动积极的企业成果。通过让 Claude 运行在基于 NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 系统、配备 NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand 网络的 Foundry 平台中，企业现在可以构建和运行更强大的智能体系统，包括能够在不同业务领域协作执行高级任务的自主子智能体和专用子智能体。

### 不断深化的合作
NVIDIA 正与 Anthropic 合作，通过将 NVIDIA 工具集成到 Anthropic 技术栈中来扩展开发者能力。这种集成使企业能够赋予 Claude 智能体特定领域的能力。借助通过 NVIDIA 加速计算实现的 NVIDIA 已验证智能体技能，企业可以将 AI 智能体深度嵌入其业务中，并将其作为组织的操作系统来使用。企业可以通过使用 NVIDIA 安全智能体工作空间参考设计，在 Azure 上运行 Claude 智能体。该参考设计提供了一个蓝图，用于在受治理的环境中运行自主智能体，在基础设施层面控制身份、网络访问、凭据和运行时策略。

### 战略合作伙伴关系的延伸
在 Microsoft Foundry 中由 NVIDIA GB300 GPU 加速的 Claude，建立在 Microsoft、NVIDIA 和 Anthropic 于去年 11 月宣布的战略合作伙伴关系之上，该合作旨在扩大企业对 Claude 的访问，并在 NVIDIA 加速计算上提供 Anthropic 模型。入门方式：访问 Microsoft Foundry 中的 Claude，并查阅 Foundry 文档了解更多信息。

history.title: Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron sued over alleged DRAM price fixing amid record memory costs — lawsuit claims coordinated HBM shift was cover to curtail DDR3 and DDR4 production
history.description: Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron were sued on June 25th in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 13 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron were sued on June 25th in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California, where 17 plaintiffs accuse the three memory makers of illegally coordinating to restrict DRAM supply and inflate prices that the complaint says have risen roughly 700% over four years. The class action, filed as Garciaguirre v. Samsung Electronics and assigned to Judge Noel Wise, invokes Section 1 of the Sherman Act and targets companies that together hold around 90% of the global DRAM market. Samsung and SK hynix have pleaded guilty to criminal DRAM price fixing once before, with the latter paying a $185 million fine in April 2005.The complaint argues that the three companies used a coordinated shift toward high-bandwidth memory (HBM), the stacked DRAM that feeds AI accelerators, as a cover to curtail production of older DDR3 and DDR4 modules. That contraction in commodity DRAM, the plaintiffs argue, pushed prices to record highs while no rival could step in: building a new DRAM fab costs tens of billions and takes years, leaving the incumbents free to cut output without fear of being undercut.The named plaintiffs include 14 individuals and three small PC businesses, among them Troy's Computers and Florida repair outfit My Florida PC, and they cite Apple's recent iPad and Mac price increases as evidence of the squeeze. They’re seeking class status, an injunction, and treble damages as a result.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: However, this case revisits a precedent that was already established in the same courthouse. A 2018 class action brought by law firm Hagens Berman made similar claims about parallel production cuts, and the district court dismissed it in 2020. The Ninth Circuit upheld that dismissal in 2022, ruling the trio's conduct was “more likely explained by lawful, unchoreographed free-market behavior” than by an illegal agreement.It was held at the time that the plaintiffs’ eight “plus factors” didn’t clear Section 1’s threshold, which demands evidence of an actual agreement rather than the conscious parallelism common in a three-supplier market. The new complaint leans on the pivot to HBM as the additional evidence that this earlier lawsuit lacked. You may like Samsung and SK hynix warn AI-driven memory shortages could last until 2027 and beyond, as HBM demand explodes Class-action lawsuit filed against HDD suspension assembly makers Team Group agrees to $1.1 million DRAM settlement in another false advertising lawsuit The three memory makers have publicly stated they’re operating independently while redirecting capacity to HBM, and senior executives have warned the shortage could run for years. Investment bank Jefferies expects DRAM prices to rise another 40% to 50% in the third quarter and a further 30% to 40% in the fourth, with no meaningful relief before 2028. The allegations remain unproven, and the defendants have not yet responded in court. history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 三星、SK海力士和美光因涉嫌在创纪录内存成本中操纵DRAM价格被起诉——诉讼称协调转向HBM是掩盖削减DDR3和DDR4生产的幌子
history.descriptionZh:
2025年6月25日，三星电子、SK海力士和美光科技在美国加州北区联邦地区法院被提起集体诉讼。17名原告指控这三大DRAM制造商合谋限制DRAM供应并哄抬价格，称相关产品价格在四年间上涨约700%。该诉讼（Garciaguirre诉三星电子案）由法官Noel Wise审理，援引了《谢尔曼法案》第1条，剑指三家合计占据全球DRAM市场约90%份额的企业。值得注意的是，三星和SK海力士曾因DRAM价格操纵刑事案认罪，其中SK海力士于2005年4月被罚款1.85亿美元。

原告的核心论点是，三家厂商利用向高带宽内存（HBM）的集体转向作为掩护——HBM是一种为AI加速器供应的堆叠式DRAM——以此掩盖对老旧DDR3和DDR4模块产量的削减。这种通用DRAM的收缩，原告认为，将价格推至历史高位，而由于新建一座DRAM晶圆厂需要耗费数百亿美元且耗时数年，没有其他竞争对手能够介入，使得现有厂商可以放心削减产量而不必担心被低价竞争。

原告包括14名个人和三家小型PC企业，如Troy's Computers和佛罗里达州的维修公司My Florida PC。他们以苹果公司近期上调iPad和Mac价格为证据，证明这种供应挤压已传导至终端产品。原告方寻求集体诉讼地位、禁令以及三倍损害赔偿。

然而，此案面临此前在同一法院确立的先例。2018年，Hagens Berman律所提起的一起集体诉讼曾提出类似的生产削减共谋指控，但地区法院于2020年驳回，第九巡回上诉法院于2022年维持了驳回决定，裁定三家的行为“更可能是合法的、无预先安排的自由市场行为”而非非法协议。当时法院认为，原告提出的八个“附加因素”未能达到《谢尔曼法案》第1条的证明门槛——该条款要求证明存在实际协议，而非三个供应商市场中常见的“有意识并行行为”。此次新的诉讼将转向HBM作为上一案件缺乏的额外证据。

三大内存厂商公开表示，它们各自独立运营，同时将产能转向HBM，其高管还警告称，短缺可能持续数年。投行杰富瑞（Jefferies）预计，DRAM价格将在第三季度再涨40%至50%，第四季度再涨30%至40%，2028年前不会出现实质性缓解。目前相关指控尚未得到证实，被告方尚未在法庭上作出回应。

history.title: Imec's 2026 roadmap details 0.3nm nodes by 2038, CFET transistors become viable at 0.7nm — company redefines Moore's Law as cell sizes gain importance for density
history.description: As CPP shrinking stalls, chipmakers find a new way to increase transistor density. Imec foresees 0.3nm in 2038, CFET insertion in 2038, HLSI era.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Imec's semiconductor process technology roadmap sets the general direction of industry development and showcases the challenges the sector is set to face over the next few decades. The roadmap gives us an idea of the timelines for the next major process nodes and transistor architectures the company will research and develop in cooperation with industry giants, such as TSMC, Intel, Nvidia, AMD, Samsung, and ASML, among many others.Imec's latest production node roadmap shows that the international research and development organization envisions 3 angstrom-class (0.3nm) fabrication technologies by 2038, but expects contact poly pitch (CPP) to stop scaling at A10 in 2030. While things might not be looking great for Moore's Law for imec, to continue scaling the chipmaker will need to adopt new technologies, such as CFET transistors and likely Hyper-NA EUV Lithography systems.GAA transistors have seven years leftAs the production of semiconductors becomes substantially more complicated, chipmakers no longer introduce all-new process technologies every couple of years. Instead, they typically roll out a new node generation every three years, with annual incremental enhancements in between. TSMC ramped N3B production in 2023, then followed it up with N3E in 2024, and N3P in 2025. Intel planned to follow the same pattern with 20A in 2024 (which was canceled), 18A in 2025, and 18A-P in 2027.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Next-generation process technologies will continue to emerge at similar cadences, according to imec's roadmap. (Image credit: Imec)Interuniversity Microelectronics Centre considers that we now live in the 2nm-class era (N2) with contact poly pitch (CPP) of around 48nm, as well as cell height of around 132nm and 6 metal tracks. The reality may be a bit different as Intel's 18A has a CPP of 50nm as well as a cell height of 160nm (high density) or 190nm (high performance), whereas TSMC's N3 can boast with a CPP of 45nm. N2 (or 18A, if you wish) will be followed by its performance and efficiency-enhanced version in the next couple of years, which is in line with how the industry has been operating in recent years. You may like IBM goes sub-1nm, develops 0.7nm-class technology Post-silicon era gets closer as industry giants crack the 2D transistor scaling bottleneck with breakthrough tech Leading-edge foundry roadmaps for TSMC, Intel and Samsung — outlining the path to 1.4nm nodes and beyond "Of course, we are going to extend our logic roadmap to the next generation beyond N2," said Julien Ryckaert, vice president of R&D at imec. "As you know, in two nanometers we have already jumped into a new technology device paradigm in the nanosheet era, and that is going to bring us deep into the Angstrom node." (Image credit: Imec)Imec expects the A14-class to emerge in 2028. TSMC expects to start high-volume manufacturing using A14 in late 2028, so the actual ramp will happen in 2029. Intel's 14A follows the same pattern. With A14, imec projects CPP to shrink to 45nm and cell height to drop to 115nm and 5.5 metal tracks. Around 2030–2031, imec expects an A10-class technology — or a 1nm-class — with a 42 nm CPP and 98 nm cell height, which will still rely on a 5.5-track architecture.It is noteworthy that gate-all-around (GAA) transistor-based nodes can be implemented either with conventional frontside power delivery networks or with backside power delivery, which reflects both imec's and TSMC's belief that BSPDN will not immediately become mandatory across all applications, as many of them do not benefit from it.It is also worth mentioning that imec expects High-NA EUV tools insertion at A14, which is in line with Intel's plans, but not with TSMC's plans.CFET insertion in the early 2030sThe roadmap becomes particularly interesting at the A7 generation, which imec expects to come in 2033. While CPP remains at 42 nm, cell height drops to roughly 80nm, and the standard-cell architecture moves to 4.5 tracks. More importantly, A7 is the point where CFET emerges as a serious candidate for production insertion. Instead of placing n-type and p-type transistors side by side, CFET stacks them vertically, which adds a third dimension to transistor scaling. What to read next TSMC's details next-gen CoWoS roadmap: over 14-reticle packages and 48x leap in compute power expected by 2029 Imec builds world's first High-NA EUV-fabricated quantum dot qubit device Analyzing TSMC's fab expansion roadmap — multi-fab N2 ramp, CoWoS, SoIC, and uncorking bottlenecks Imec’s roadmap explicitly positions CFET as the leading contender for A7, which means that the organization sees conventional nanosheet architectures approaching practical scaling limits in the early 2030s. Yet, since A7's CPP does not change from A10, chipmakers may or may not adopt the all-new transistor architecture at A7. Also note that imec seems to consider BSPDN as mandatory for CFETs. (Image credit: Imec)"Moving into A7, the seventh-angstrom generation, which is the fourth generation of nanosheet, we see more and more challenges in scaling the conventional nanosheet device technology," Ryckaert said. "There is a contender that we have already mentioned as well in previous presentations, where CFET could start emerging as the solution for the next era of transistors."Beyond A7, the roadmap seems to depend on CFET evolution. The A5 generation, expected in 2035–2036, retains a 42nm CPP but reduces cell height to about 64nm using a 4-track library. By 2038, the roadmap reaches A3 with a 39nm CPP and 50nm cell height. At this point, imec envisions sequential CFET implementations and eventually bonded CFET structures that further exploit vertical integration. In fact, vertical integration seems to be the new way we should look at Moore's Law's evolution. Meanwhile, to get to a 39nm CPP and 50nm cell height, chipmakers might need to use Hyper-NA EUV lithography scanners, according to imec.Redefining Moore's Law (Image credit: Imec)The most interesting thing about imec's roadmap is that it essentially redefines what Moore's Law means. Traditionally, we consider Moore's Law as the observation that the number of transistors on a chip of a certain size doubles every 18 – 24 months, as they are getting smaller.The fact that imec shows CPP stuck at 42 nm from A10 through A5 is almost an admission that classical transistor scaling is running out of steam, and future density gains must come from vertical integration. In the imec roadmap, transistors are still getting denser, but not exactly because individual transistors are shrinking at the same pace they used to decades ago, but because chip designers can fit more logic gates into a given area because of different transistor architectures, 3D integration, or backside power delivery.As a result, in the coming years, we may no longer care how many nanometers a gate pitch is, or individual transistors, but rather the size of a standard cell. After all, when companies like AMD, Intel, or Nvidia design a chip, they do not place individual transistors, but actual blocks built from standard cells. Yet, calculating the size of a standard cell is complicated because while cell height is fixed, its width is not, and depends on the actual function.Library height × CPP is not the size of a specific standard cell. It is the fundamental footprint unit of a standard-cell library and a widely used proxy for logic density. Actual standard cells have that height, but their width varies depending on function. Instead, the industry uses such metrics as logic cell area (standard-cell footprint) — Cell Height × CPP — that measures the actual footprint of the logic building blocks that designers use, not just the dimensions of individual transistors.The transition from 6-track cells at N2 to 3-track cells at A3 illustrates how future density gains will rely as much on shrinking standard-cell height as on reducing transistor pitch. As a result, despite the fact that CPP shrinkage is expected to stall for years, logic cell area is set to decrease; designers will be able to extract transistor density gain from future nodes, proving that Moore's Law is still here.Hetrogenous Large-Scale Integration × Cross-Technology Co-OptimizationGiven all the changes that the semiconductor industry is already experiencing and what is set to come, imec believes the sector is entering a new era that it calls Heterogeneous Large-Scale Integration (HLSI). The concept reflects a shift away from traditional VLSI scaling, where progress largely depended on the evolution of transistors and increasing transistor density, toward a model that combines multiple technologies within a single compute platform.Future systems will rely on heterogeneous integration of logic, memory, power-delivery circuitry, and optical I/O using advanced 3D and 3D + 2.5D packaging technologies, according to imec's predictions. Of course, the organization expects AI workloads to become the main driver of semiconductor demand, so expect both compute architectures and the semiconductor industry to evolve in a direction that satisfies the needs of AI applications."As we will move deeper into AI-driven architecture, we will need to double down on the heterogeneity that technology offers, and this will probably move the VLSI paradigm to the HLSI paradigm, the Heterogeneous Large Scale Integration," Ryckaert said.To optimize future platforms on the system level rather than develop individual components in isolation, imec has established its Cross-Technology Co-Optimization (XTCO) framework, which could be seen as an integral part of the HLSI vision. XTCO is designed to wed development logic, memory, interconnects, power delivery, cooling, and packaging, and assesses their impact on key system metrics such as compute density, energy efficiency, thermal performance, and memory.It remains to be seen how this is going to work out, if at all, given the fact that logic process technologies are developed at foundries, memory technologies are designed at DRAM makers, whereas cooling is developed at third parties like CoolIt or Frore Systems.Power and coolingAs individual chips get denser and more power-hungry, power delivery is set to become a critical bottleneck, which is why all leading chipmakers — Intel, Samsung, and TSMC — are implementing or set to implement backside power delivery technologies and integrated voltage regulators.Imec expects future AI accelerators and CPUs to rely on a combination of BSPDN, IVRs, embedded capacitors, and advanced power semiconductors to reduce losses and improve efficiency. Over time, more power-conversion stages are expected to migrate from racks and motherboards into packages themselves to deliver cleaner power directly to transistors.Since we are talking about multi-chiplet packages consuming kilowatts of power, the importance of cooling is hard to overestimate. For sure, 3D stacking and CFETs will not make cooling any easier because thermal power density is set to increase linearly with the number of transistors, thermal resistance is set to increase, and local hotspots will become an even bigger problem than they are today. As a result, imec expects future compute platforms to rely on a combination of more advanced cooling technologies, improved heat spreading, fine-grained thermal sensors, and system-level thermal optimization techniques. "At the end of the day, what we need to achieve is a reduced energy cost of data movement. We need to improve the TDP for better thermal management," Ryckaert said. "We need to improve the efficiency of the power delivery, and we need to obviously increase the compute density to improve the functionality."In short, useful future scaling will depend not only on the ability to build transistors and increase their density, but on delivering power efficiently and removing heat effectively.Paving the path forwardImec's latest semiconductor roadmap projects logic process technologies all the way to A3 generation around 2038 and argues that Moore's Law can continue despite the slowing pace of traditional transistor scaling. While the Dennard scaling for semiconductors is over, there are plenty of interesting things incoming.According to the roadmap, conventional gate-all-around nanosheet transistors should remain viable through A10, while CFET architectures become a candidate for production insertion at the A7 generation around 2033. Meanwhile, future transistor density gains are expected to come from vertical integration, reduced standard-cell footprints, and eventually sequential and bonded CFET structures rather than from aggressive shrinking of transistor dimensions.
history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: Imec 2026年路线图：到2038年实现0.3nm节点，0.7nm时CFET晶体管可行——公司重新定义摩尔定律，单元尺寸对密度愈发重要
history.descriptionZh:
imec（比利时微电子研究中心）发布了最新的半导体工艺技术路线图，为整个行业未来数十年的发展指明了方向，也揭示了芯片制造将面临的严峻挑战。这份路线图由imec与台积电、英特尔、英伟达、AMD、三星、ASML等业界巨头合作研发，明确了未来主要工艺节点和晶体管架构的时间表。

**路线图概览：从2nm到3埃米时代**

imec认为我们目前正处于2nm级（N2）时代，其接触多晶硅间距（CPP）约为48nm，单元高度约为132nm，采用6个金属轨道。但实际参数因厂商而异：英特尔18A工艺的CPP为50nm，单元高度为160nm（高密度）或190nm（高性能）；台积电N3工艺的CPP为45nm。

imec预计，**A14（14埃米级）将于2028年问世**。台积电计划在2028年底开始A14的高量产，真正爬坡则在2029年；英特尔14A也遵循相同节奏。A14的CPP将缩至45nm，单元高度降至115nm，采用5.5个金属轨道。

**到2030-2031年，A10（1nm级）将登场**，CPP为42nm，单元高度98nm，仍采用5.5轨道架构。值得注意的是，基于GAA（全环绕栅极）晶体管的节点既可采用传统正面供电网络，也可采用背面供电网络（BSPDN）。imec和台积电均认为BSPDN不会立即在所有应用中成为强制要求，因为许多应用并不能从中受益。另外，imec预计High-NA EUV光刻工具将在A14节点引入，这与英特尔计划一致，但台积电尚未跟进。

**CFET晶体管：2030年代初的正式引入**

路线图最引人注目的部分是**A7（7埃米级，预计2033年）**。虽然CPP仍维持42nm，但单元高度将骤降至约80nm，标准单元架构变为4.5个轨道。更重要的是，A7是**CFET（互补场效应晶体管）作为量产候选方案**的起点。与传统将n型和p型晶体管并排放置不同，CFET将它们垂直堆叠，从而增加晶体管缩放的第三维度。imec明确将CFET定位为A7的主要候选，这意味着传统的纳米片架构在2030年代初将接近实际缩放极限。由于A7的CPP与A10相同，芯片制造商也可能选择不采用全新晶体管架构。imec认为BSPDN对于CFET是强制性的。

**A5（预计2035-2036年）** 和随后**A3（预计2038年）** 的演进依赖于CFET的发展。A5仍保持42nm CPP，但单元高度降至约64nm，采用4轨道库。到A3，CPP缩减至39nm，单元高度降至50nm。此时，imec设想了顺序式CFET实现以及最终的键合式CFET结构，进一步利用垂直集成。同时，要实现39nm CPP和50nm单元高度，芯片制造商可能需要使用**Hyper-NA EUV光刻设备**。

**摩尔定律的重新定义**

imec路线图最有趣的一点是它实际上重新定义了摩尔定律。传统上，摩尔定律是指芯片上晶体管数量每18-24个月翻一番。但imec显示从A10到A5，CPP将卡在42nm，这几乎等于承认传统晶体管缩放正在失去动力，未来的密度增长必须来自垂直集成。在imec路线图中，晶体管密度仍在增加，但并非因为单个晶体管尺寸以过去同样的速度缩小，而是因为不同的晶体管架构、3D集成或背面供电，使得芯片设计人员能在给定面积内塞入更多逻辑门。

因此，未来我们可能不再关注栅极间距或单个晶体管的纳米数，而是关注标准单元的尺寸。从N2的6轨道单元到A3的3轨道单元，这种转变说明了未来的密度增长将同样依赖于缩小标准单元高度和减小晶体管间距。尽管CPP缩放在几年内停滞，逻辑单元面积仍将减小，设计人员仍能从未来节点中获得晶体管密度增益，证明摩尔定律仍在延续。

**异构大规模集成（HLSI）与跨技术协同优化（XTCO）**

面对这些变化，imec认为行业正在进入一个称为**异构大规模集成（HLSI）**的新时代。这个概念反映了从传统VLSI缩放（主要依赖晶体管演进和密度增加）向在一个计算平台内融合多种技术的模型转变。未来系统将依赖于逻辑、存储器、供电电路和光学I/O的异构集成，使用先进的3D和3D+2.5D封装技术。人工智能工作负载将成为半导体需求的主要驱动力，计算架构和半导体行业都将朝着满足AI应用需求的方向发展。

为了在系统层面优化未来平台，imec建立了**跨技术协同优化（XTCO）**框架。该框架旨在将逻辑、存储器、互连、供电、冷却和封装的开发结合起来，评估它们对计算密度、能效、热性能和内存等关键系统指标的影响。然而，由于逻辑工艺由代工厂开发、存储器由DRAM制造商设计、冷却由第三方公司开发，这一框架如何实际运作仍有待观察。

**供电与散热的挑战**

随着单个芯片越来越密集、功耗越来越高，供电成为关键瓶颈。这就是所有领先芯片制造商（英特尔、三星、台积电）都在实施或计划实施背面供电技术和集成稳压器（IVR）的原因。imec预计未来的AI加速器和CPU将依赖BSPDN、IVR、嵌入式电容器和先进功率半导体的组合来降低损耗、提高效率。越来越多的电源转换阶段将从机架和主板转移到封装内部，以便将更清洁的电力直接输送到晶体管。

由于我们正在谈论功耗达到千瓦级的多芯片封装，散热的重要性怎么强调也不为过。3D堆叠和CFET并不会使散热更容易，因为热功率密度将随晶体管数量线性增加，热阻会增加，局部热点问题将比今天更严重。因此，imec预计未来的计算平台将结合更先进的冷却技术、改进的散热技术、精细温度传感器和系统级热优化技术。最终目标是要降低数据移动的能耗成本，改善热管理，提高供电效率，并增加计算密度以提升功能。

**总结：前行的道路**

imec的最新半导体路线图将逻辑工艺技术展望到2038年的A3代，并论证了尽管传统晶体管缩放速度放缓，摩尔定律仍可延续。虽然半导体的登纳德缩放已经结束，但仍有大量值得期待的新技术涌现。根据路线图，传统的GAA纳米片晶体管将在A10节点之前保持可行，而CFET架构将在2033年的A7节点成为量产候选。未来的晶体管密度提升将主要来自垂直集成、减小的标准单元面积，以及最终顺序堆叠和键合式CFET结构，而非激进的晶体管尺寸缩小。整个行业正从单纯的晶体管缩放转向包含供电、散热、异构集成的系统级优化，以支撑AI时代的计算需求。

history.title: Open Models, Closed Environments: Palantir Brings Secure AI to US Agencies With NVIDIA Nemotron
history.description: Showcasing the importance of open source innovation in American AI, Palantir’s new intelligent engine — introduced today — uses NVIDIA Nemotron open models to serve the needs of U.S. government agencies. Open source software has long been a pillar of U.S. technology leadership.  In 1969, DARPA connected four university computers — from UCLA, Stanford, UCSB […]
history.content: Showcasing the importance of open source innovation in American AI, Palantir’s new intelligent engine — introduced today — uses NVIDIA Nemotron open models to serve the needs of U.S. government agencies. Open source software has long been a pillar of U.S. technology leadership. In 1969, DARPA connected four university computers — from UCLA, Stanford, UCSB and the University of Utah — laying the infrastructure backbone that became the internet. In those early days, U.S.-led open source contributions also drove leadership in coding languages, with UNIX in 1969 and C at Bell Labs in 1972. These languages led to more open source software building on those foundations, including the Linux Kernel in 1991, GitHub in 2008 and Docker in 2013. Today, open models are making frontier-level AI broadly accessible, with control over customization and trust through transparency. They give enterprises and government agencies the ability to inspect, adapt and deploy AI in sensitive environments, making them essential for national security, corporate sustainability and industrial innovation. With domain-optimized harnesses, strong open models can deliver frontier capabilities while helping customers retain control over proprietary data, model weights and deployment environments. Today’s Palantir announcement brings NVIDIA Nemotron open models into air-gapped environments — secure setups that are completely isolated from unsecured networks — on NVIDIA accelerated computing. Palantir will use NVIDIA Nemotron open models to build custom frontier-quality models to serve the U.S. government. Many of the government’s operations mirror private-sector enterprises — including commerce, energy, healthcare, agriculture, education and transportation. With about 3 million civilian employees, the U.S. government is essentially one of the world’s largest enterprises. Providing critical services across so many disciplines is incredibly complex. AI can help streamline this complexity and boost insights to drive productivity. From food safety to maintaining safety on interstate highway infrastructure, AI can help government agencies tackle operational challenges, just like American public sector businesses. With this new engine, agencies and operators can run customized Nemotron models on their own infrastructure, train on their own data and retain full ownership of the resulting models — including the weights that encode their operational knowledge. Palantir’s Sovereign AI Operating System — built on AIP, Ontology, Foundry and Apollo — handles the operational and data authorization layer for easy deployment in sensitive environments. Explicit data authorization, architecturally enforced isolation and full auditability are already central to Palantir’s Sovereign AI Operating System. As these customized models are used in production, agencies and operators can continually improve them within their own environments using new data and feedback. This creates a data flywheel that continually optimizes model performance while keeping data, models and auditability under customer control. While NVIDIA Nemotron open models provide a customizable and continually learning model layer on Palantir’s Sovereign AI Operating System, enterprise-grade deployments can be supported through the NVIDIA AI Enterprise software suite. Open Models Support Trust, Access, Control and Lower Costs Together, NVIDIA Nemotron open models and Palantir’s critical infrastructure products enable trust, accessibility, control and lower costs. Trust through transparency: Through independent review of open models, researchers can identify vulnerabilities, biases and unintended behaviors that a single organization may miss. This visibility then allows for models to be refined to address these issues, improving safety through transparency. Customization and control: Companies, governments and developers can modify and fine-tune open models to better suit their use cases. Open models can be deployed in regulated environments, like in the financial industry, where closed models might breach data security or other privacy laws. Lower costs fuel economic development: Open models are in broad use, with about two-thirds of companies already using them and reporting on their cost efficiency. Organizations note that these cost savings are an important factor as they build AI that can scale with success. The combination of NVIDIA Nemotron open models with Palantir’s critical infrastructure products — AIP, Foundry, Ontology and Apollo — bolsters U.S. technology leadership for government agencies and commercial businesses alike. Running the models on air-gapped NVIDIA-powered infrastructure keeps the data and models secure — and ready to support the most important missions. Learn more about NVIDIA Nemotron and the Palantir Sovereign AI Operating System Reference Architecture with NVIDIA.
history.author: Justin Boitano
history.titleZh: Open Models, Closed Environments: Palantir Brings Secure AI to US Agencies With NVIDIA Nemotron
history.descriptionZh:
Palantir 公司近期推出了一款基于 NVIDIA Nemotron 开放模型的全新智能引擎，旨在为美国政府机构提供先进的人工智能服务。这一举措凸显了开源创新在美国人工智能领域的重要地位，开放模型正成为国家技术领导力的关键组成部分。

开源软件长期以来都是美国技术领先的基石。1969年，DARPA将加州大学洛杉矶分校、斯坦福大学、加州大学圣巴巴拉分校和犹他大学的四台大学计算机连接起来，奠定了后来互联网的基础架构。早期，美国主导的开源贡献还推动了编程语言的突破，例如1969年的UNIX和1972年贝尔实验室开发的C语言。这些语言催生了更多开源软件，如1991年的Linux内核、2008年的GitHub和2013年的Docker。如今，开放模型让前沿AI变得广泛可及，通过透明性实现定制化控制和信任，使企业和政府机构能够在敏感环境中审查、调整和部署AI，这对国家安全、企业可持续性和工业创新至关重要。借助领域优化的框架，强大的开放模型能够提供前沿能力，同时帮助客户保留对专有数据、模型权重和部署环境的控制。

Palantir 此次宣布将 NVIDIA Nemotron 开放模型引入气隙环境——一种与不安全的网络完全隔离的安全设置——并运行在 NVIDIA 加速计算平台上。Palantir 将使用 NVIDIA Nemotron 开放模型构建定制的、达到前沿质量水平的AI模型，专门服务于美国政府。美国政府许多运作与私营企业相似，涵盖商业、能源、医疗、农业、教育和交通等领域。拥有约300万文职雇员的美国政府实际上是世界上最大的企业之一。在如此多的领域提供关键服务极为复杂，AI有助于简化这种复杂性并提升洞察力以实现生产力增长。从食品安全到州际高速公路安全维护，AI可以帮助政府机构应对运营挑战，正如美国公共部门企业所经历的那样。

通过这款新引擎，政府机构及其操作人员可以在自己的基础设施上运行定制的 Nemotron 模型，用自己的数据训练模型，并完全拥有最终模型的所有权——包括编码了其运营知识的权重。Palantir 的主权AI操作系统基于 AIP、Ontology、Foundry 和 Apollo 构建，负责运营和数据授权层，便于在敏感环境中部署。显式数据授权、架构性隔离以及完全可审计性已经是 Palantir 主权AI操作系统的核心特性。当这些定制模型投入生产后，机构可以利用新数据和反馈在其内部环境中持续改进模型，从而形成一个数据飞轮，不断优化模型性能，同时保持数据、模型和审计性完全在客户控制之下。NVIDIA Nemotron 开放模型提供了可定制且持续学习的模型层，部署在 Palantir 的主权AI操作系统上，而企业级部署可以通过 NVIDIA AI Enterprise 软件套件获得支持。

开放模型支持信任、可访问性、控制和低成本。NVIDIA Nemotron 开放模型与 Palantir 的关键基础设施产品相结合，能够实现信任、可访问性、控制和更低成本。信任来源于透明性：通过独立审查开放模型，研究人员可以发现单一组织可能忽略的漏洞、偏见和意外行为，这种可见性使得模型得以优化以解决这些问题，从而通过透明性提高安全性。定制与控制：企业、政府和开发者可以修改和微调开放模型以更好地适应其使用场景，开放模型可以在受监管的环境（如金融行业）中部署，而封闭模型可能违反数据安全或隐私法律。低成本推动经济发展：开放模型被广泛使用，约三分之二的企业已经使用它们并报告了成本效益。组织指出，这些成本节省是构建可扩展AI的重要因素。

NVIDIA Nemotron 开放模型与 Palantir 的关键基础设施产品（AIP、Foundry、Ontology 和 Apollo）的组合，增强了美国在政府机构和商业企业中的技术领导力。在基于NVIDIA的气隙基础设施上运行模型，确保了数据和模型的安全性，并随时准备支持最重要的任务。了解更多关于 NVIDIA Nemotron 和 Palantir 主权AI操作系统参考架构的信息，请访问相关资源。
